name: Rufus Reid
abstract: Rufus Reid (born February 10, 1944, in Atlanta, Georgia) is an American jazz bassist, educator, and composer.

interviews.title: Rufus Reid interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, GA, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: my friend Rufus Reid, who is a great bassist and was running the jazz studies program at William Paterson College at the time, was going to take a leave of absence for a year and he asked me if I would take his place.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Soph
about_quotes.evidence: Rufus Reid and I have played off and on together for years. It’s got to be close to 40 years now that we’ve known each other.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Soph interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 9, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Genevieve Rose
about_quotes.evidence: I met Rufus Reid for like a minute a couple of years ago at a Massachusetts Music Educators’ Convention.
about_quotes.doc_title: Genevieve Rose interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Carl Allen
about_quotes.evidence: when I was a student of his at William Paterson... I said, 'Rufus, you will always be my teacher.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Carl Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Atlanta, Georgia, January 4, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Vax
about_quotes.evidence: we recorded it at SONY Studios in New York City and I flew in and Reggie Thomas, the piano player, flew in, and it was Rufus Reid on bass who lived there
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Vax interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: And that’s called “Chasing the Bird” and that’s with Pepper. It’s all Gershwin. And Pepper Adams and Joe Puma, Mel Lewis and Rufus Reid.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002
